symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly enlarging, exquisitely painful hot lump on posterior thigh

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid, painful, hot swelling is typical of a pyogenic abscess.
Heat and pain also occur in cellulitis; a discrete lump favours a collection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Central fluctuant pointing area with impending rupture
symptoms.character: soft, shiny, pointing centre; fluctuant on palpation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fluctuant pointing collection defines a drainable abscess and mandates incision and drainage.
Fluctuance indicates a collection, distinguishing abscess from flat cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: New fever and shivery malaise (patient-reported home temperature 38)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: cutaneous abscess with early systemic involvement
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic features mark a transition from local to systemic infection and drive the same-day/antibiotic decision and sepsis watch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe pain unrelieved by paracetamol and ibuprofen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: High-tension collection pain is typically poorly responsive to simple oral analgesia until drained.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured tachycardia (HR 110) and low-grade fever (38.0) with otherwise stable observations

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: cutaneous abscess with early systemic involvement
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician vitals confirming early systemic response; BP 140/85 and SpO2 99% argue against septic shock. The AI Doctor infers systemic involvement from the patient's fever/malaise, not from a measured HR.

symptoms.symptom_name: Purulent discharge on incision growing Staphylococcus aureus (potentially MRSA) on culture

investigation_details.investigation_name: Wound swab microscopy, culture and sensitivity (M/C/S)
investigation_details.expected_result: Isolation of Staphylococcus aureus, potentially methicillin-resistant (MRSA)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Antibiotic choice not tailored to sensitivities; MRSA missed if empirical cover inadequate.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frank pus on incision confirms the abscess; culture identifies the organism and MRSA status. Only obtainable in person, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: No red streaking, no rigors, no confusion, no collapse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of spreading erythema, systemic toxicity and disproportionate features keeps this at urgent same-day rather than emergency; their appearance would flip the tier to T5.

symptoms.symptom_name: No penetrating injury, bite, or known immunosuppression

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spontaneous abscess in an immunocompetent host; absence of immunosuppression lowers (but does not remove) the threshold for complications.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Fluctuant pointing collection with early systemic features
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: cutaneous abscess requiring urgent same-day incision and drainage
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these establish a drainable abscess that has begun to involve the patient systemically — the trigger for same-day source control rather than a routine booking.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab M/C/S taken at the time of in-person incision and drainage
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies the organism and MRSA status to tailor antibiotics; taken by the in-person clinician at drainage, not orderable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine venous bloods / inflammatory markers in the well, afebrile-trending patient
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required to manage an uncomplicated drainable abscess with early systemic features that is going straight to same-day in-person source control; bloods are for the in-person clinician to decide if sepsis is suspected.
